concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Ways to store graph data, typically using adjacency lists or matrices.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores neighbors level by level.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A First-In, First-Out (FIFO) data structure used in BFS.

concepts.name: Distance
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges in a path between two nodes.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: The path with the fewest edges between a source and a target node.

concepts.name: Unweighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where all edges have the same implicit weight (usually 1).

concepts.name: Source Node
concepts.short_definition: The node from which the shortest path is calculated.

concepts.name: Target Node
concepts.short_definition: The node to which the shortest path is being sought.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection to keep track of nodes already processed during traversal.

concepts.name: Level Order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting nodes in a graph layer by layer, characteristic of BFS.
